---
title: "Fish Alpha Diversity"
output: html_document
---
```{r Required Libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(ggpubr)
library(microbiome)
library(indicspecies)
```
# Alpha Diversity Plot

The alpha diversity for this dataset will be calculated using the Shannon Index and compared pairwise using the Wilcoxon's ranked-sums test. This script will use the phyloseq object created previously.

```{r Preliminary Data Analysis}
# Set the seed
set.seed(1)

# Load ps

ps = readRDS('../datasets/ps_filtered.rds')

# View histogram
hist(sample_sums(ps))

### Rarefied phyloseq object

# Find sampling depth
# Run without labels to be able to view all curves
rarecurve(
  t(
    data.frame(ps@otu_table)
  ),
  step=1000,
  label = FALSE
)

### Rationale ###
# I will use a sampling depth of 2525 based on the output of the rarecurve() function
# and my results from Qiime.
#################

# Rarefy data
psRare = ps %>% 
  rarefy_even_depth(sample.size = 2525, rngseed = 1)

table(sample_sums(psRare))
```

```{r}
# Sort data into Fast, Moderate, and Slow

# Filter to hindgut samples only 
hindgut = subset_samples(ps, sample_type == "hindgut")

# Subset the phyloseq object by swim performance group (Fast, Moderate, Slow)
# performance.fast = subset_samples(hindgut, swim_performance %in% c('accelerator', 'crusier sprinter', 'manoeuvrer'))
# performance.slow = subset_samples(hindgut, swim_performance %in% c('flow refuging', 'burrowing'))
# performance.moderate = subset_samples(hindgut, swim_performance == 'generalist')

```
With the rarefied data, we can now calculate alpha diversity (Shannon) and Wilcoxon's test.

```{r Alpha Diversity}
# Calculate Shannon

fishShannon = plot_richness(
  hindgut,
  x = 'swim_performance',
  measures = 'Shannon',
  color = 'swim_performance'
)

# Make it publication-ready
pData = fishShannon$data
str(pData)

### HELP SETTTING UP THE FILTERING
# ?ifelse

fast = c('accelerator','crusier sprinter','manoeuvrer')
#moderate = c('generalist')
#slow = c('flow refuging', 'burrowing')

grouped = pData %>%
  mutate(swim_group = ifelse(swim_performance %in% fast,
                      'Fast',
                      ifelse(swim_performance == 'generalist',
                      'Moderate',
                      'Slow'
                      )
  ),
  .after = swim_performance
  )


fishFormatted = grouped %>% 
  ggplot(aes(swim_group,value,fill = swim_group))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA)+
  geom_jitter(height = 0,width = 0.2)+
  theme_classic(base_size = 18)+
  facet_wrap(~variable,ncol=7,scales = 'free_y')+
  ylab('Alpha Diversity')+
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45,vjust=1,hjust=1))+
  labs(fill='Swim Performance')

# Now add the Wilcoxon element
# comparisonsList = list(
#   # Accelerator
#   c('accelerator','burrowing'),
#   c('accelerator','crusier sprinter'),
#   c('accelerator','flow refuging'),
#   c('accelerator','generalist'),
#   c('accelerator','manoeuvrer'),
#   # Burrowing
#   c('burrowing','crusier sprinter'),
#   c('burrowing','flow refuging'),
#   c('burrowing','generalist'),
#   c('burrowing','manoeuvrer'),
#   # Crusier Sprinter
#   c('crusier sprinter','flow refuging'),
#   c('crusier sprinter','generalist'),
#   c('crusier sprinter','manoeuvrer'),
#   # Flow Refuging
#   c('flow refuging','generalist'),
#   c('flow refuging','manoeuvrer'),
#   # Generalist
#   c('generalist','manoeuvrer')
# )

comparisonsList=list(
  c('Fast','Moderate'),
  c('Fast', 'Slow'),
  c('Moderate', 'Slow')
)

fishCompare = fishFormatted +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisonsList, method = 'wilcox.test',size = 6)
fishCompare
```

The code above gives us a raw alpha diversity plot; ```fishCompare``` gives us a plot with p-values comparing all swim performances. We will simplify the plot by removing P-values that are above significance (P>0.05) to make the figure more publication-friendly.

```{r Making It Clean}
# Add on to fishFormatted from fishCompare

# cleanAlphaFish<-fishFormatted+
#   ylim(0,6.75)+
#   geom_signif(
#     comparison = list(
#       c('burrowing','flow refuging')),
#     y_position = c(
#       5.5
#     ),
#     annotations = c(
#       '*'
#     ),
#     textsize = 16
#   )+
#   scale_x_discrete(labels=
#     c(
#       'Accelerator',
#       'Burrowing',
#       'Crusier Sprinter',
#       'Flow Refuging',
#       'Generalist',
#       'Manoeuvrer'
#     )
#   )+
#   scale_fill_discrete(
#     labels=
#     c(
#       'Accelerator',
#       'Burrowing',
#       'Crusier Sprinter',
#       'Flow Refuging',
#       'Generalist',
#       'Manoeuvrer'
#     )
#   )

cleanAlphaFish<-fishFormatted+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c('#8E7CA6','#E07A9A','#fcba65'))
cleanAlphaFish
```
Now that we have a clean figure, we can export it as a ```.jpg```.

```{r Export Figure}
ggsave('../plots/alphaCleanFishGrouped.jpg',
       plot = cleanAlphaFish,
       height=8.5, width = 14)
```
